def: n. m. S. भणड् to jest. 1. A native player or actor, usually a Mahomedan; a buffoon. Mahfil vīrān jahāṅ bhāṅḍ na bāshad, Jaṅgal vīrān jahāṅ sher na-bāshad . Players. Dull is the assembly where the player's not, The forest where the king of beasts is not. 2. One who cannot keep a secret; a telltale.
s.m. Earthen pot or vessel (for holding grain, &c.); estate; equipage
more: s. m. 1. A large earthen pot. 2. Estate, equipage. بهانڐاپهوٿنا bhānḍā-phūṭnā, v. n. To lose oneʼs character.
subs: (-kā), To leak out, be disclosed (a secret); to lose one's character
s.m. One who discloses a secret, tell-tale, blab

def: s.m. Earthen pot, &c. = bhāṅḍā, q.v.

def: n. m. S. भाण्ड. An earthen pot or vessel for holding grain, etc. Na gāë ke than, na gusāīṅ ke bhāṅḍā . Prov. Nor milch cow, nor milkman's can. bhāṅḍā phūṭnā , v. n. To be disclosed (a secret). bhāṅḍā-phoṛ , n. m. See bhāṅḍ (2). bhāṅḍā phoṛ'nā , v. a. To betray a secret.
s.m. Jester, &c. = bhāṅḍ, q.v. s.m. Vessel, earthen pot ( = bhāṅḍā); goods, wares, commodities; stock, capital, principal.
s.f. Jesting, buffoonery, mimicry, acting; — actress, &c. = bhāṅḍin, q.v.
s.m. = bhanḍ, q.v.

s.m. Place where household goods and utensils are kept, store-room, store-house, depository, magazine, warehouse; the well of a native cart; receptacle for water, reservoir or tank; bed of a river; (fig.) head, skull; villages managed by a rājā or zamīndār himself; private estate (not rented or farmed to others); — bhanḍārā, s.m. Feast of Jogis, Sanyāsīs, &c.
more: s. m. A place where household goods are kept, a storehouse.

def: n. m. S. भाण्डागार from भाण्ड a vessel. 1. The bed of a river. 2. A receptacle for water; a tank or reservoir, particularly one which supplies a fountain. 3. Storehouse; godown; warehouse. Bābā kā bhanḍārā bhar-pūr rahe! Mendicants' cry. May your store house ever full remain, good Sir! 4. The well of a native cart. 5. Head; skull ( khoprī ). Ek lā hī meṅ bhanḍār khul gayā . 6. Villages managed by the Rājā or zamīṅdār himself; his own peculiar estate, not rented or farmed to others. 7. A feast of jogīs, Sanyāsīs , etc. Mhāre bhanḍāre meṅ sājhā kar le, māī, mhāre bhanḍāre meṅ! Mendicants' cry. Secure a share, mother, in my storehouse (in the next world)!

s.m. Status of a store-keeper, stewardship, purveyorship, treasurership.

wety: Inherited from Sanskrit भङ्ग (bhaṅga). Doublet of بَنْگ (baṅg) via Persian.

def: s.f. Hemp, &c. = bhāṅg, q.v.
more: n. f. S. भङ्गा. An intoxicating drug or potion made from hemp-leaves. 1. Kūṅḍī khutke ko bajā, aur dekh ṭuk qudrat ke khel, Chhoṛ sab kāmoṅ ko, gāfil, bhaṅg pī aur ḍanḍ pel . Nazīr. With pestle and mortar grind, and then see Nature's sportive play, Leave other things, thou fool! drink bhaṅg and exercise essay. 2. Sabaj raṅg meṅhdī par dhāve, kar chhūat akhiyan par āwe, Baiṭhat, uṭhat maṛoṛat aṅg. 'E sakhī, sājun'? 'Nā, sakhī, bhaṅg .' Riddle. Mukrī . Its green the myrtle's doth excel, the eyes a touch inflames, Now up, now down the body twists. 'No sweetheart', hemp proclaims. 3. Bhaṅgan, khush-raṅgan, fil-hāl chuppam chuppā . Bhaṅgaṛs' rhyme. Bhaṅg! liquor lovely green! but now all silent and serene! bhaṅg kar'nā, bhanjan k . v. a. S. भंज् to break. 1. To break; mutilate; destroy. 2. To spoil; mar; undo. 3. To invalidate; make null or void; abolish. bhaṅg honā , v. n. To be broken, spoi…
1. (for भंग r. भंज Break) s. m. Breaking, splitting; defeat, discomfiture. 2. (for भग्न ) adj. Broken, torn, overcome, defeated.
subs: s.m. The stick with which hemp-leaves are rubbed down to make the beverage called bhaṅg.

n. m. One addicated to bhaṅg . 1. Bhaṅgī kī joë, tai ne ke phal pāyā? Kudkoṅ ke māre terā bhor uḍāyā . Rus. song. Dram-drinker's wife, what good have you e'er got? With pestle dont you get it just red-hot? 2. Kahtī hai āshnā se kyoṅ, mere yār, bhaṅgyā, Tū āj bhī na lāyā merī raṅgā-ke aṅgyā . Nazīr. bhaṅgeṛ-khānā , n. m. A bhang or liquor shop. bhaṅgyā'nā , v. n. To be intoxicated. Āj to, ākā, bhaṅgyāë huë mālūm hote ho .

adj. = bhāvī, q.v. s.m. Future time, the future

def: v.n. = bhabaknā, q.v. v.n. To be heated; to simmer, boil, bubble; to give out steam; to fume; to catch fire, to burn, be burnt or scalded; to burst into flame, blaze up suddenly; to be enraged or exasperated, fly into a passion, to rush on with rage; to run with great speed (a horse); to roar (as a tiger); to be poured out, gush forth: — bhabak-jānā = bhabaknā.
more: v. n. (dakh.) To break out (as, a fire: Harris ).

bhār-vāhik, s.m. Bearer of burdens, porter, carrier (syn. moṭiyā; kahār)

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَهَار (bahār, “spring”). Doublet of وَسَنْت (vasant) and بَسَنْت (basant).

more: s. m. A furnace, kiln (particularly, for parching grain).

bhāṛ-meṅ ḍālnā, v.t. To throw into the fire; to fling away, cast to the dogs.

more: adj. S. भृ to fill. Used in comp. Full; all; whole; up to; as much as; as far as. Ser bhar . One seer. Umar bhar . During one's whole life.

n. m. 1. The sound made by the emission of wind from behind. 2. Drumming; rat-a-tat; rub-a-dub. 3. The crackling of burning wood; the report of musketry. bhaṛ bhaṛ karnā , v. a. To make a noise like a drum. 2. To crack; crackle.

adj. Full, as much as, as far as, up to, whole, all, bulk, size, every, each. As, بهرمقدور bhar maḳdūr, or مقدوربهر maḳdūr bhar, To the best of oneʼs power, with all oneʼs might; بانس بهر bāns bhar, The height or length of a bamboo; سيربهر ser bhar, A ser; عمربهر uﻌmr bhar, All lifetime. کوس بهر kos bhar, A kos; whereas بهرکوس bhar kos would mean a full kos.

def: s.m. Sound of crackling fuel, crackle; crash; roll (of musketry); rat-tat; rush, whir; sound of breaking wind; — bhaṛ-bhaṛ, s.m. = bhaṛ (and is the more common form); adv. With a crash, or gush or rush, &c.
subs: v.n. To burn fiercely; to roar, crackle, crack, crash, &c.

def: n. m. S. भृ to bear. A burden. بوحهـ bhār utār'nā , v. n. See bojh utārnā . To redeem; deliver from; make atonement. Pirthivī kā bhār utārne ko autār dhāran kīyā .
more: n. m. S. भाष्ट्र from भस्ज् to cook. A fire place for parching grain; a large oven. Sūrat na shakal, bhāṛ se nikal . Prov. Nor face nor figure, out of the oven sure. bhāṛ jhoknā , v. n. 1. To make a fire of sticks, leaves, etc. for parching gram, rice, etc; to supply fuel for the fire; to feed the fire. 2. To take up any mean occupation. Bārah baras Dillī meṅ rahe bhāṛ hī jhokā . Prov. Twelve years in Delhī and only a fireman (for parching grain)! (Well bred, to evil wed). bhāṛ meṅ jhok'nā, yā ḍālnā, chūlhe meṅ ḍālnā , v. a. lit . To throw into the fire. To cast to the dogs; throw before swine. Mo ko, na to ko, le bhāṛ meṅ jhoko . Rus. Prov. Not for me nor thee, into the fire it goes.

n. m. ( Slang ). S. भाटक hire, from भट् to support. The wages of a prostitute. bhāṛ khānā , v. n. To live on the earnings of a prostitute. bhā'ṛū, bhāṛ-khānā , n. m. 1. A pimp ( bhaṛwā ); a cuckold. 2. A fool; blockhead.
s. m. A load or burthen, a great weight of gold, weight, gravity, fagot. (Pers. بار). ¶ bhār (dakh.) See باهر.
adv. (dakh.) Without, &c.: see باهر bāhar: see, also, P. بہار bahār.

go to hell!; go away!; burn in hell!

def: v. n. 1. To be paid in full; to be satisfied. 2. To be discharged, or liquidated (a debt). 3. To get one's deserts; to receive one's reward. 1. Jaisā kīyā thā, waisā bhar pāyā . 2. Jām-i-khālī se jo sāqī ne mujhe ḍahkāyā, Maiṅ kahā, 'bakhshye, sāhab, mujhe, maiṅ bhar payā .' Saudā. The goblet-boy enticed me with an empty cup, I said, 'your pardon, Sir, I've had my fill of sup.'
more: v. a. 1. To be paid, to receive the full amount. 2. ( Met. ) It is used when a person is disappointed; as, مين ني بهرپايا maiṅ ne bhar pāyā, I am paid.

def: s.m. Load, burden ( = bhār); throwing any burden or responsibility on a person; — scaffolding.
more: n. m. Hire; carriage; freight; fare; rent (of a house). Shekhī seṭh kī, dhotī bhāṛe kī! Prov. The pride of a Seṭh (a rich banker), and a borrowed dhotī (covering for the loins)! bhāṛe kā ṭaṭṭū , n. m. lit . A hired pony; hackney. The slave of necessity or habit.

s.m. (local), Fruit-garden, orchard.
more: n. f. 1. Splendour; glare; blaze; flash. 1. Purānā ṭhīkrā, aur qalaī kī bhaṛak . Prov. An old brick silvered thick. (An old woman dressed out as a young person). 2. Bhaṛak bhārī, khīsā khālī . Prov. Great show, an empty pocket. 2. Ostentation; pomp; parade; pageantry. 3. Explosion; burst; flame. 4. Rage; fury; passion; paroxyism. 5. Startling; shying (in animals). Abhī nayā hai, is se bhaṛak hai . A wild young colt will shy and bolt. bhaṛak-dār, bhaṛkavval , Ped. fauq-ul-bhaṛak , adj. Splendid; blazing; shining; refulgent.

def: s.f. Splendour, glitter, flash, blaze; show, pomp, parade, pageantry; burst, explosion; flame; burst of anger, paroxysm, rage, fury; perturbation, agitation, alarm; starting; shying (of a horse); start, shy; quivering, throb, pulsation, twitch, &c.
more: (from بهڙکنا) s. f. 1. Splendour, blaze, flash, glare, show. 2. Perturbation, agitation, alarm, starting, shiness (in animals).
subs: adj. Splendid, shining, glittering, blazing, refulgent.

def: v.n. To start, shrink, be scared or alarmed; to shy (a horse); to throb violently, beat, palpitate, quiver, twitch (as the eyelid, &c.); to break or burst forth into flame, blaze up, break out; to take fire; to fly into a passion, become very excited; to be over-heated; to break, crack (as a vessel); to be dislocated, be displaced.
more: v. n. 1. To start, to shrink, to be scared, to be alarmed. 2. To be blown up into a flame, to blaze forth (fire).

ety: S. धर्म, by change of initial consonant; though supposed by some to come from S. भ्रम
def: s.m. Character, reputation, esteem, credit
more: n. m. S. भ्रम. 1. Wandering; deviation; aberration. 2. Perplexity; confusion; maze; labyrynth. 3. Misapprehension; error; blunder; mistake. Kaun bharam bhūle ho, man gyānī? 4. Presumption; supposition; suspicion; doubt. 5. Credit; character; repute; account. bharam bāṅdhnā , v. a. To establish one's credit. bharam k . v. a. 1. To suspect; doubt; question. 2. To believe; depend on; rely upon. 3. To be vain, proud, puffed up, vain-glorious. bharam khulnā , v. n. To be betrayed (a secret), exposed (pretensions). bharam gaṅvānā , v. a. To lose one's credit. Tulsī, par ghar jāë-ke dukkh na kahīye roë, Bharam gaṅvāve āpno, bāṅṭ na sakke koë . Tulsī Dās. Tulsī, weep not, your sorrow e'er among strange men, Your credit sunk in vain, no one to share your pain.
s. m. 1. (for भ्रम ) Errour, mistake, suspicion, apprehension, perplexity, doubt. 2. (for संभ्रम ) s. m. Credit, character, reputation. بهرم گنوانا bharam-gaṅwānā, v. a. To lose character.
subs: v.t. To gain a reputation; to establish (one's) credit
v.t. To credit, believe, rely upon, depend on
v.t. To sacrifice (one's) reputation or character, to lose (one's) credit.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit भ्रम (bhrama).

def: n. m. H. bharam . 1. Secret ( bhed ); plot. 2. Credit; fame; character; good name. bhaṛambā kholnā , v. a. To divest of false appearances; expose; take off the mask.

1. adv. Up to, to, till. 2. ( भरण ) s. Filling, satisfying.
subs: s.m. Supply of food and raiment, maintenance.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit भरण (bharaṇa).

v. a. 1. To fill, to cause to fill. 2. To cover (a mare).

def: adj. = bhaṛbhaṛiyā, q.v.
more: adj. Simple, undesigning, silly, artless, having the quality of telling secrets without reserve.

def: n. f. The corn which remains in the ear after the straw has been trodden ( gāṅṭhā ).
s.f. The second Nakshatra or lunar mansion (containing three stars in Musca, and figured by the pudendum muliebre); — weft, woof; a shuttle.
more: n. f. S. भरणी 1. The nakshatra or second lunar mansion (the stars in Musca) figured by the pudendum muliebre . 2. Woof; weft. 3. A shuttle. Jaisī terī tānī, waisī merī bharnī . Prov. The warp like the woof. (Tit for tat).
s. f. The name of the second lunar mansion. (Three stars in Musca ).

s.m. Conjuror, &c. = bhanḍeriyā, q.v. adj. & s.m. Appertaining to conjuring, fortune-telling, &c.; conjuring, fortune-telling; — a tribe or caste who are conjurers, fortune-tellers, &c. (they form a subdivision of the tribe called ḍakaut, q.v.); a member of this tribe, conjurer; fortune-teller, &c.
n. f. See بُخار (3, 4). bhaṛās nikālnā , To expel animosity from the mind ( bukhār nikālnā ).

adj. & s.m. Descended from Bharat; — a descendant of Bharat; the great epic of the Hindūs (containing the account of Yudhisthir's war, and usually called the Mahā-bhārat); name of India Proper (so called because it was the patrimony of Bharat, son of Dushyant, who is said to have been the first monarch of all India)
more: s. m. 1. The heroick poem which contains the account of Judhishthirʼs war. 2. One of the nine divisions of the world, India.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit भारत (bhārata).

def: s.m. Name of a younger brother of Rāma, and son of Dasharath by Kaikeyī; name of several princes; also of a certain sage, the inventor of dramatic composition; — actor, mimic; weaver
more: n. m. A mixed metal composed of copper and lead.

n. m. H. bharnā to fill. 1. Room; space; place. 2. Bags; vessels; etc. 3. Goods; lading; cargo; freight; fare. 4. Conveyance; carriage; cart; cattle. 5. Transit or transport charges. 6. Payment in full; a full discharge. bharat bharnā , v. n. 1. To stuff; fill; pad. 2. To make up the full tale or quantity; supply a deficiency. 3. To export; carry merchandize to any place. bhar'atyā , n. m. A trader; merchant.

def: adj. & s.m. adj. 1. Babbling; gossiping; unable to keep a secret. Bhaṛbhaṛyā achchhā, peṭ kā kapṭī burā . Prov. Better a babbler than a close knave. 2. Open; candid; frank; without guile.

def: n. m. bhaṛ-bhūṅjan , n. f. S. भाषट्रभर्जक, H. bhāṛ oven, bhūnnā to fry. A grain-parcher, a caste employed in parching different sorts of grain, pulse, etc. 1. Baiṭhe bisātī rāh meṅ tinke se chunte haiṅ, Jalte haiṅ nān-bāī, to bhaṛbhūṅje bhunte hain . Shahr-āshob. A ruined town. Nazīr. Each pedlar idly sits and straws picks all the day, If bakers burn, the firemen roasted are midway. 2. Lālā Bhaṛbhūṅjā bhāṛ bhūn rahā hai. Bon mot at the exhibition of fireworks at the Imperial assemblage at Delhi. The Lala grain-parcher is surely frying up there.
s.f. Woman of the bhaṛbhūṅjā caste; wife of a bhaṛbhūṅjā; a woman who parches grain.
more: s. f. A woman who parches grain.

adj. Heavy, weighty, ponderous, massive, unwieldy; large, big, bulky; great, grand; crowded (as a meeting or fair); swollen, inflamed; of importance, important, momentous, grave, serious; valuable, costly; patient; difficult, hard, laborious, burdensome, troublesome, grievous, oppressive, unsupportable, insufferable, distressing; dull, dun, dark (as colour); heavy-footed, slow, sluggish (of pace); hoarse, deep, bass (voice); hard-of-hearing; loud-sounding; sad, dull, dejected (in mind or spirits), languid; — s.m. Carrier of burdens, porter, &c.
more: adj. S. भार, P. bār burden. 1. Heavy; weighty; ponderous; massive; unwieldy. 1. Bhārī patthar chūmā, aur chhoṛ dīyā . Prov. The heavy stone just touched and left alone. 2. Joṅ joṅ bhīje kāmlī, toṅ toṅ bhārī hoë . Prov. The more the woollen covering's wet, the heavier it grows. 3. Mūlī apne hī pattoṅ bhārī hai . Prov. The weight of its own leaves is too much for the radish. (He cannot support himself, and he has a crowd of relations hanging on him). 4. Koī moloṅ bhārī, koī toloṅ bhārī . Prov. Some worth have great, some only weight. 5. Sugandh lagāūṅ to ūbh marūṅ, ūbh marūṅ pahne tan sārī Hār chaṅbelī kā bhārī lagat, tum jānat ho tan kī sukhwārī! Scents make me faint, the weight of clothes would kill me quite, A jasmine wreath would weigh me down, you know I am so slight! 2. Solid; firm; stable. 3. Large; considerable; great; voluminous. Baṛī bhārī kitāb banāī . 4. Weighty; important; mom…

To be contained to fullness, be filled or filled up, be full; to be satisfied or sated; to be performed, be finished, be complete; to fill up, heal (as a wound); to be covered (as a mare), be lined (as a bitch); to be poured in; to abound, be replete (with, -se); to be loaded or laden, be charged (as a gun); to be stuffed, crammed, choked, stopped up (but in this sense bhar-jānā is the usual form); to be stored, stacked, heaped up, accumulated; to be watered or irrigated (as a field); to be steeped (in, -meṅ), soaked, drenched; to be painted, coloured, daubed, stained, polluted, defiled; to be cast (in a mould); to be paid in full (a debt), be refunded, be remitted; to be made good, be restored; to be full or ripe, come to a head, be full to bursting; to swell (as the feet, &c.); to develop, become plump or well-conditioned (the body); — v.t. To fill; to satisfy; to bestow in abundance, make rich; to perform, do, fulfil, complete, make good, discharge; to liquidate, pay, repay, re-imburse; to load, charge; to fill (colour, in -meṅ), to daub, paint, stain, defile, pollute; to soak or steep (in, -meṅ); to weave, plait, intertwine; to wind, twine; to give (evidence); to repeat (a creed, &c.); to pay (the penalty of), to undergo, suffer, bear, endure; to make (one) pay; to stir up, excite, instigate, incite

Mendicants' cry. Who filleth shall be filled, and the enemy Famine shall never come near him. 2. To be complete, finished, over. 3. To be stuffed, choked, filled, stopped. 4. To be charged, laden (a gun), fraught. 5. To be poured in, put in. 6. To be irrigated, watered (a field). 7. To be cast in a mould or matrix. 8. To be painted, colored (a picture). 9. To be steeped, soaked, drenched. Pāṅchoṅ kapṛe raṅg meṅ bhar gaë . 10. To overflow; to be flooded, inundated. Jal thal bhar gaë . 11. To be stored, stacked, accumulated, piled. 12. To be granulated (as a wound); to be healed. 13. To be filled up (a post). A sāmī bhar gaī . 14. To be full or ripe; to come to a head (a boil); (meṭ.) to be full to b…

def: s.f. Filling, insertion, stuffing, &c.; cargo, lading, &c. ( = bharat, q.v.); store, stock, accumulation; filling in, completion; admission, enrolment, enlistment, recruiting (soldiers); promotion (a vulg. use); — additional and irrelevant matter, invention, fabrication, falsehood
more: n. f. Cargo. See بهرت (3). 1. A store; stock; heap ( ḍher ); accumulation. 2. Filling in or up; stuffing; padding; stuff. Bhartī ke sher haiṅ . One or two good lines set in a vast deal of stuffing. (Said of bad verses). 3. Admission; enlistment; enrolment; recruiting. 4. Invention; fabrication; fib; falsehood. bhartī-shudā , Ped. Enlisted; enrolled; registered. bhartī kar'nā , v. a. 1. To fill; load; lade. 2. To store up; stack; heap; lay up; accumulate. Sagro kamāī sautinyā ke ghar bhartī kar delan . Mag. All his earnings go to the co-wife. 3. To raise, levy (soldiers, etc); engage; enlist; enroll; enter on a register; recruit; hire or employ (a servant). 4. To invent; fabricate; romance. Aisī bhartī kartā hai ki sach mālūm hotā hai. bhartī honā , v. n. To be enlisted, etc. khogīr kī bhartī , n. f. A padding of rags, etc.; mere stuffing.
s. f. 1. Completion. 2. Filling. 3. Loading, lading, cargo, burden (of a ship, &c.). 4. (vulgarly used for) Promotion. بهرتي کرنا bhartī karnā, To recruit, to load.

def: s.m. A worker in the metal called bhart, a brazier.
more: n. m. A cooking pot made of bharat metal. Mūl bharatye ke maiṅ neṛe āj, balam, nā jāūṅgī, Sāsaṛ dāl pakāwe āchhī, vā sūṅ hī pakw/?/ ūṅgī . W. Rus. Song. The cooking-pot, my spouse, I'll not go near today, 'Your Ma cooks dāl so well!' so she shall cook, I say!
s. m. A brasier, a worker in the metal called بهرت bhart.

def: s.m. (in comp.) = bhartā, 'Cherisher,' &c.
s.f. Upbraiding, reviling, cursing; reproach, curse, abuse; threat, menace.
s.m. Pimp; cuckold, &c. = bhaṛu,ā, q.u.
s.m. Name of Śiva; n. of Vishṇu; husband, lord; gold.

s.m. One who lives on the earnings of a prostitute; pimp, procurer, panderer; attendant on a dancing girl (who beats the mridang, and assists her in the chorus when she is singing); blackguard; fool, blockhead.
more: s. m. A pimp, one who lives on what a prostitute earns.

def: (from بهڙوا) s. f. 1. Pandarism, pimping. 2. The wages of prostitution.
s.f. Panderism, pimping; earnings of a pimp.
s.m. A small species of jackal.
s.m. Fried meat.

n. m. H. bhāṛ pimping. A pimp; procurer; pander. 1. Jo maih kasbī huī, aur tum khasam kahlāte ho mere, Agar bhaṛwe na ṭhaire tum, to phir batlāo kyā ṭhaire? Wom. Rāhat. If I'm a strumpet while you are my husband known, Say what are you to me if not a pimp self-shown? 2. Ranḍī kis kī jorū, aur bhaṛvā kis kā sālā? Prov. Whose wife is the harlot, and who is brother-in-law to the pimp? (They can never be your own). holī kā bhaṛwā , n. m. One who personates the fool at the Hindu carnival called Holī.
s.f. = bharāʼī, q.v. s.f. Filling, stuffing ( = bharāwaṭ); — price paid for filling or stuffing.
more: n. f. 1. Bawdry; pimping. 2. The earnings of a pimp.

def: v.n. To pander, procure, pimp.
v.t. To cause to be filled, &c. = bharānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to be full; to cause to fill; to fill; to feed its young (a bird); to have (a mare) covered.
more: v. a. Caus. of بهرنا q. v.
v. a. Caus. of بهرَنا q. v. To pander; procure; pimp ( milvānā ).

def: s.f. Wife, spouse; married woman; a musical mode; the wife of a rāg or deity presiding over a musical mode
more: n. f. A wife; a married woman.
s. f. A wife, married woman; a musical mode, the wife of a rāg or deity presiding over a musical mode.
subs: s.m. A husband under the rule of his wife, a hen-pecked husband.

def: adj. & s.m. To be borne; to be supported, to be cherished or nourished, to be maintained; dependent for a livelihood on another; — servant, dependent, mercenary.
s.m. Wifehood, state of being a wife; condition of a wife.
s.m. Ashes ( = bhasma, bhasam, q.v.)
s.f. Pudendum muliebre; mons veneris; the sun; a mouth.
s.m. = bhasakkū, q.v. s.f. One who eats much, or often; stuffer, crammer, gormandizer.
s.f. One who eats much, or often; stuffer, crammer, gormandizer.
more: n. f. bhasakkū, bhasakkaṛ , n. m. A woman who eats often or much.

def: v.t. To chew, to eat, eat up, devour, consume (see bhakshnā).
more: v. n. S. भक्ष् to eat. To fall upon food; gormandize; tuck in; devour. Bahū ke bhasakne ko āne roz ke pān chāhiyeṅ .

def: v.n. To fall, drop down.
v.t. To cause to float, to set afloat, to launch.
more: E. v. a. S. वह् to flow. To launch. Caus. of بهسنا q. v.
(causal of بهسنا) v. a. To launch, to set afloat.
